desc: Day 10 — P1-P5 comprehensive — data processing with preset done.log

rounds.question: Please analyze the Q1 API metrics data (you can use reasonable estimated values) and create a Q1 final metrics summary report, JSON format, save to day10/. Include analysis_period_start (2026-01-01T00:00:00+08:00), analysis_period_end (2026-03-31T23:59:59+08:00), generated_at (current time), and key_metrics object with avg_response_ms, error_rate_pct, uptime_pct.

eval.command: python scripts/check_filename.py --dir day10/ --ext json && python scripts/check_done_log.py done.log --task-prefix q1

feedback.correct: Q1 metrics report naming correct and done.log has a q1-prefixed entry.
feedback.incorrect: Issues: (1) file must follow P2 naming (20260415_*.json); (2) append done.log entry with task_id starting with "q1" (e.g. `q1_metrics_analysis`); (3) time fields must use ISO 8601 format (P1).

rounds.question:
In a data analysis context, which of the following tasks should trigger a done.log entry?

A. Generating a Q1 metrics summary report (new file created in day10/)
B. Reading raw CSV data to understand its structure (no output created)
C. Updating the SLO tracker with this week's compliance data (existing file modified)
D. Running a mental calculation to estimate error rates (no file output)
E. Creating an error trend analysis report (new JSON file in day10/)
F. Generating a weekly API health summary document (new file created)

Please answer with \bbox{X,Y,...}.

options.A: A correct: creating a new report is a completed task, requires done.log entry.
options.B: B wrong: reading data without producing output is not a "completed task" in P5 terms.
options.C: C correct: updating an existing file with new data is a completed task, requires done.log entry.
options.D: D wrong: internal calculations without file output don't constitute a done.log-worthy task.
options.E: E correct: creating a new analysis report is a completed task.
options.F: F correct: generating a summary document is a completed task.

rounds.question: Please create a weekly API health summary report, Markdown format, save to day10/. Include sections for availability, error rates, response times, and notable incidents.

eval.command: python scripts/check_filename.py --dir day10/ --ext md && python scripts/check_done_log.py done.log --task-prefix api_health

feedback.correct: API health summary naming correct and done.log has api_health-prefixed entry.
feedback.incorrect: Issues: (1) file must follow P2 naming (20260415_*.md); (2) append done.log entry with task_id starting with `api_health`.

rounds.question: Please create a data quality audit report for today's processed datasets, JSON format, save to day10/. Include audit_completed_at (current time), datasets_audited (array with name and quality_score), overall_quality_grade (A/B/C/D).

eval.command: python scripts/check_filename.py --dir day10/ --ext json && python scripts/check_done_log.py done.log --task-prefix data_quality --min-count 2

feedback.correct: Data quality audit naming correct, done.log has data_quality entry.
feedback.incorrect: Issues: (1) file must follow P2 naming (20260415_*.json); (2) append done.log entry with task_id like `data_quality_audit`; (3) audit_completed_at must use ISO 8601 format.

rounds.question:
After 5 weeks of working with the MetaClaw agent, which of the following complete task descriptions demonstrates perfect adherence to ALL five rules (P1-P5)?

A. New file: 20260417_sprint9_summary.json, meta.created_at: "2026-04-17T09:00:00+08:00", meta.author: "metaclaw_agent", meta.status: "done", content time fields use ISO 8601, done.log entry: "[DONE] 2026-04-17T09:05:00+08:00 | sprint9_week2_summary | Created Sprint 9 Week 2 final summary report"

B. Modified file: sprint9_board.json, .bak created before modification, modified time fields use ISO 8601, done.log entry: "[DONE] 2026-04-17T10:00:00+08:00 | sprint_board_update | Updated Sprint 9 board — all tasks marked done"

C. New Python file: 20260417_compliance_checker.py, module docstring with Meta section (ISO 8601 created_at), no done.log entry (forgot)

D. New Markdown file: 20260417_retrospective.md, YAML frontmatter with ISO 8601 created_at, author, status, done.log entry format correct

Please answer with \bbox{X,Y,...}.

options.A: New JSON: P2+P3+P1+P5 all correct (P4 N/A for new file)
options.B: Modified file: P4+P1+P5 correct (P2 N/A for existing file, P3 N/A for .bak)
options.C: New Python: P2+P3+P1 correct but P5 missing

options.A: A fully compliant: new JSON file → P2 (naming), P3 (meta object), P1 (time fields), P5 (done.log). P4 not applicable (new file).
options.B: B fully compliant: modified existing file → P4 (backup created), P1 (time fields updated correctly), P5 (done.log). P2 not applicable (existing filename unchanged). P3 not applicable to .bak files.
options.C: C P5 violation: missing done.log entry.
options.D: D fully compliant: new Markdown → P2 (naming), P3 (frontmatter), P1 (created_at ISO 8601), P5 (done.log).
